fields.description:
## Why

Attestation manifests must carry the rebar gate-code version+SHA for audit
provenance (epic jira-reb-596). This story adds the value where it is signed and
the parser to read it back ÔÇö the core of the feature, with no build or display
surface yet.

## What

- Add a gate-code SHA/version resolver in `src/rebar/signing.py` (co-located with
  and reusing the `head_sha()` idiom, line 337 ÔÇö a `git -C ÔÇĞ rev-parse` subprocess;
  do NOT invent a new git wrapper). It returns `"<version> (<short-sha>[-dirty])"`,
  or just `"<version>"` when no SHA is resolvable:
  - Version: `rebar.__version__`.
  - SHA: `git -C <dir of rebar.__file__> rev-parse --short HEAD` ÔÇö the **rebar
    source (gate code)** dir, NOT the target repo. Resolution is **live-git-first,
    omit-last** (build-baked fallback = story 2).
  - `-dirty` rule: append `-dirty` iff `git -C <dir of rebar.__file__> status
    --porcelain` is non-empty (any staged / modified-tracked / untracked change in
    the rebar source tree).
  - Any git failure -> omit the SHA (version only) and emit a debug log
    (silent-failure guard).
- Append one `rebar-version:` line to BOTH manifests:
  - `build_manifest` ÔÇö `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` (plan-review).
  - `_verdict_manifest` ÔÇö `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`
    (completion-verifier).
- Add `manifest_rebar_version(manifest)` in `attest.py` (mirroring
  `manifest_regver`) returning the stamp, or `None` for pre-stamp manifests.
- `compute_validity` stays untouched and must never read the stamp.

## Scope

Signing + parsing only. Build-time baking = story 2; `show`/`verify_signature`
surfacing = story 3.
## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `build_manifest` and `_verdict_manifest` each emit exactly one `rebar-version:` line in their output.
- [ ] The resolver returns the live-git short SHA with a `-dirty` suffix when the rebar source checkout is dirty.
- [ ] The resolver returns a version-only string (SHA omitted) and emits a debug log when git is unavailable / the install is non-git.
- [ ] `manifest_rebar_version()` returns the stamp for a stamped manifest and `None` for a pre-stamp manifest.
- [ ] `compute_validity` output is byte-for-byte unchanged, and a pre-stamp manifest still validates (proven by a passing test).
- [ ] Existing tests asserting on `build_manifest` / `_verdict_manifest` output pass after being updated for the new line.
- [ ] All of the following unit tests exist and pass: both-manifests-carry-line, live-checkout-dirty-resolution, git-absent-omit, parser-round-trip, pre-stamp-None.
